raw_data.details: The "Primary Education Scholarship" scheme by the Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides a state scholarship of ₹150 for 10 months at the rate of ₹15 per month to students from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities studying in classes 1 to 5. The scholarship distribution is managed through the Directorate of Public Education.
raw_data.benefits: Scholarship of ₹150 for 10 months at the rate of ₹15 per month.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a student. The applicant should be from a [De-Notified](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Public/Pages/TribesDetail? ID=2),[Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Public/Pages/TribesDetail? ID=1) Community.*The applicant should not need to apply again for renewal while continuing in classes of the same rate (e.g., classes 1 to 5).*The applicant should be eligible for the scholarship even if they fail one academic year.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineRegistration
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Navigate to the top right corner of the homepage and click "[Registration".](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Registration/Default)
Step 3: On the registration page, provide the mandatory details. (the fields marked with * are mandatory). If required, verify your Mobile Number and/or Email ID via OTP. Finally, click "Register". Application
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Use the credentials you created during registration to log in.
Step 3: Navigate to the online application form of the scheme you intend to apply for.
Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size.
Step 5: Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections.
Step 6: Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any).
Step 7: Click the "Submit" to submit your application.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size PhotoAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste Certificate Previous Class MarksheetSamagra IDBank Account Details

The scholarship distribution is managed through the Directorate of Public Education. BenefitsScholarship of ₹150 for 10 months at the rate of ₹15 per month. EligibilityThe applicant should be a student. The applicant should be from a [De-Notified](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Public/Pages/TribesDetail? ID=2),[Nomadic, or Semi-Nomadic](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Public/Pages/TribesDetail? ID=1) Community.*The applicant should not need to apply again for renewal while continuing in classes of the same rate (e.g., classes 1 to 5).*The applicant should be eligible for the scholarship even if they fail one academic year. Application ProcessOnlineRegistrationStep 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)". Step 2: Navigate to the top right corner of the homepage and click "[Registration".](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Registration/Default)Step 3: On the registration page, provide the mandatory details. (the fields marked with * are mandatory). If required, verify your Mobile Number and/or Email ID via OTP. Finally, click "Register". ApplicationStep 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)". Step 2: Use the credentials you created during registration to log in. Step 3: Navigate to the online application form of the scheme you intend to apply for. Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Step 5: Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections. Step 6: Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any). Step 7: Click the "Submit" to submit your application. Documents RequiredPassport-size PhotoAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste Certificate Previous Class MarksheetSamagra IDBank Account DetailsFrequently Asked QuestionsWhich department manages this scholarship scheme? The Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, manages it, and it is distributed through the Directorate of Public Education. What documents do I need to submit with the scholarship application? You need to submit a passport-size photo, Aadhaar Card, domicile certificate, caste certificate, previous class marksheet, Samagra ID, and bank account details. How do I register for the scholarship on the Samarth Portal? Visit the Samarth Portal, click "Registration" in the top right corner, provide mandatory details, verify your contact information if required, and click "Register."If my child fails a class, will they lose the scholarship? No, students remain eligible for the scholarship even if they fail one academic year. Do I need to apply for renewal every year while my child is in primary school? No, you don't need to reapply while continuing in classes 1 to 5. Who is eligible for the Primary Education Scholarship? Students from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities studying in classes 1 to 5 are eligible. How much scholarship money will my child receive under this scheme? The scholarship amount is ₹15 per month for 10 months, totalling ₹150. Are students beyond class 5 eligible for this scholarship? No, the scholarship is specifically for students studying in classes 1 to 5 only. Do I need to fill all fields marked with an asterisk (*)? Yes, all fields marked with * are mentioned as mandatory in the registration process. Is the scholarship amount same for all classes from 1 to 5? Yes, the scholarship amount remains ₹15 per month across all eligible classes. Sources And References[Scheme Details](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Public/Pages/SchemeDetail? ID=1)

faqs.question: Which department manages this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: The Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, manages it, and it is distributed through the Directorate of Public Education.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to submit with the scholarship application?
faqs.answer: You need to submit a passport-size photo, Aadhaar Card, domicile certificate, caste certificate, previous class marksheet, Samagra ID, and bank account details.

faqs.question: How do I register for the scholarship on the Samarth Portal?
faqs.answer: Visit the Samarth Portal, click "Registration" in the top right corner, provide mandatory details, verify your contact information if required, and click "Register."

faqs.question: If my child fails a class, will they lose the scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, students remain eligible for the scholarship even if they fail one academic year.

faqs.question: Do I need to apply for renewal every year while my child is in primary school?
faqs.answer: No, you don't need to reapply while continuing in classes 1 to 5.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Primary Education Scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities studying in classes 1 to 5 are eligible.

faqs.question: How much scholarship money will my child receive under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount is ₹15 per month for 10 months, totalling ₹150.

faqs.question: Are students beyond class 5 eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, the scholarship is specifically for students studying in classes 1 to 5 only.

faqs.question: Do I need to fill all fields marked with an asterisk (*)?
faqs.answer: Yes, all fields marked with * are mentioned as mandatory in the registration process.

faqs.question: Is the scholarship amount same for all classes from 1 to 5?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scholarship amount remains ₹15 per month across all eligible classes.
